keyword: stop chatgpt from adding comments to every code block
title: Tired of Deleting Comments? Stop ChatGPT from Commenting Every Code Block
problem_description: Users have to manually tell AI 'no comments' in every single new chat session, wasting time on repetitive negative prompting.
how_to_solve: Use a /recall-no-comments hook to instantly inject your strict 'no-comment' code style into any chat session.

keyword: how to keep nextjs tailwind context in new claude chat
title: Don't Retype Your Stack: How to Keep Next.js & Tailwind Context in Claude
problem_description: Starting a new session in Claude requires re-pasting the same 'I use App Router, Tailwind, and TypeScript' prompt every time.
how_to_solve: Create a /recall-stack-web hook that auto-fills your environment specs with one command.

keyword: chatgpt forgot my project business logic again
title: Fixing 'Memory Amnesia': Why ChatGPT Forgets Your Project Logic and How to Solve It
problem_description: The built-in memory feature is often too vague or fails to recall deep business rules in new threads.
how_to_solve: Inject the full 2000-word business logic instantly using a /recall-logic-v2 hook via NeverExplain extension.

keyword: fastest way to paste coding standards into ai
title: Beyond Copy-Paste: The Fastest Way to Sync Coding Standards with AI
problem_description: Developers waste 30 seconds every chat session finding and pasting their team's .eslintrc or naming conventions.
how_to_solve: NeverExplain's Chrome extension monitors your input for /recall-style and replaces it with your full team standard.

keyword: share llm context between chatgpt and claude
title: Cross-Platform AI Context: Syncing Your Project Background Across ChatGPT & Claude
problem_description: Users frequently switch between LLMs (ChatGPT for logic, Claude for refactoring) and hate re-explaining the project to both.
how_to_solve: Use a unified /recall hook that works identically on chatgpt.com, claude.ai, and deepseek.com.

keyword: custom instructions for multiple projects chatgpt
title: The Solution to ChatGPT's Single-Custom-Instruction Limitation
problem_description: ChatGPT only allows one set of global custom instructions, but developers work on 5+ projects with different stacks.
how_to_solve: Replace the static custom instructions with dynamic project hooks like /recall-proj1, /recall-proj2.

keyword: stop ai from using outdated library syntax
title: Prevent AI Hallucinations: Force LLMs to Use Your Specific Library Versions
problem_description: LLMs often use deprecated APIs (e.g., Next.js Pages router instead of App router) unless explicitly told in every chat.
how_to_solve: Auto-inject your package.json dependencies and version requirements via a simple hook.

keyword: how to tell claude my naming conventions once
title: One Click Naming Conventions: How to Never Explain Your Style to Claude Again
problem_description: Users are annoyed by AI using camelCase when the project requires snake_case, requiring constant correction.
how_to_solve: Set a /recall-naming hook that injects specific naming rules into the first prompt of every session.

keyword: boilerplate prompt for shadcn ui components
title: Get Perfect Shadcn UI Code: The Ultimate Auto-Fill Context
problem_description: Generating Shadcn components requires telling the AI where the components folder is and which icons to use (Lucide vs Radix).
how_to_solve: Bind your Shadcn configuration to /recall-shadcn to ensure every UI generation is build-ready.

keyword: automate ai project onboarding for new developers
title: AI-Powered Team Onboarding: Syncing Project Context Across the Whole Team
problem_description: New team members don't know the exact 'AI prompts' used to maintain consistency in the project.
how_to_solve: Share a central /recall-master-context link so every team member's AI follows the same architecture.

keyword: v0 dev repetitive prompting problem
title: Better V0.dev Results: How to Inject Deep Context into V0 Prompts
problem_description: V0.dev is great but often lacks the specific brand guidelines or design system context that is tedious to type.
how_to_solve: Use NeverExplain to expand brand-style hooks inside the V0.dev input field.

keyword: chatgpt memory full workarounds for devs
title: Is Your ChatGPT Memory Full? Here is a Cleaner Way to Store Project Context
problem_description: Users hit the memory limit or find the memory becomes 'polluted' with old project info.
how_to_solve: Offload your context to NeverExplain and call it on-demand with hooks, keeping your AI memory clean.

keyword: how to feed long api documentation to ai every time
title: Stop Pasting API Docs: Auto-Recall Documentation in New Chats
problem_description: When working with niche or private APIs, users have to paste the docs repeatedly so the AI doesn't hallucinate.
how_to_solve: Store the API spec in NeverExplain and use /recall-api-docs to ground the AI's response.

keyword: avoid ai code bloat by setting context
title: Kill AI Code Bloat: Automatically Inject 'Minimalist' Coding Principles
problem_description: AI tends to write overly verbose code unless told to be 'concise and DRY' in every new session.
how_to_solve: Enforce a /recall-dry hook to ensure the AI always produces production-grade, minimalist code.

keyword: efficient way to manage multiple system prompts
title: Multiple System Prompts? How to Switch Between Roles with Hooks
problem_description: Switching between a 'Senior React Dev' persona and a 'Database Architect' persona is manual and slow.
how_to_solve: Toggle roles instantly with /recall-senior-dev or /recall-db-pro hooks.

keyword: how to feed database schema to cursor ai every time
title: Stop Hallucinating Columns: The Fast Way to Feed Database Schemas to AI
problem_description: When asking AI to write SQL or Prisma queries, it constantly guesses column names that don't exist unless you paste the schema in every chat.
how_to_solve: Store your SQL/Prisma schema in NeverExplain and call it with /recall-schema to ground the AI in your actual data structure.

keyword: tell chatgpt to use tailwind v4 instead of v3
title: Exhausted by Deprecated Classes? How to Force ChatGPT to use Tailwind v4
problem_description: AI models often default to older version syntax (like Tailwind v3) even when your project is on the latest version, leading to broken styles.
how_to_solve: Use a /recall-tw4 hook to inject the latest documentation and class rules into every design-related prompt.

keyword: inject brand voice into claude artifacts
title: Consistent Branding in Claude: How to Automate Brand Voice Injection
problem_description: Claude's writing is great but often lacks specific brand 'vibe' or forbidden words, requiring manual editing every time.
how_to_solve: Create a /recall-voice hook that contains your brand's style guide and tone of voice for instant application.

keyword: stop ai from using arrow functions in react
title: Enforcing Code Style: How to Make AI Write Function Declarations Only
problem_description: Teams with strict coding styles (e.g., preferring 'function' over 'const' for components) waste time refactoring AI-generated code.
how_to_solve: Set a /recall-react-style hook that explicitly forbids arrow functions and other non-compliant patterns.

keyword: manage multiple client personas in chatgpt
title: Freelancer Hack: Switching Client Contexts in ChatGPT Without Copy-Paste
problem_description: Freelancers working for 5 different clients have to constantly remind the AI: 'Client A uses this tone, Client B uses that tech stack'.
how_to_solve: Assign each client a hook like /recall-client-a, /recall-client-b to switch context in 1 second.

keyword: tell ai to use biome instead of prettier
title: Stop Getting Prettier Suggestions: Switching AI to Biome.js Context
problem_description: AI keeps suggesting Prettier/ESLint configs when the project has moved to Biome, causing configuration conflicts.
how_to_solve: Use /recall-biome to explain your linting and formatting rules to the AI once and for all.

keyword: chatgpt custom instructions for separate projects
title: Why ChatGPT Needs Per-Project Custom Instructions (And How to Get Them)
problem_description: Users are frustrated that Custom Instructions are global. Changing them for every project is a UX nightmare.
how_to_solve: NeverExplain acts as a 'Switcher' for custom instructions via simple slash commands in the chat bar.

keyword: keep api documentation updated in ai context
title: Outdated AI Knowledge? How to Inject Your Private API Docs Instantly
problem_description: LLMs don't know your company's internal API changes made last week. Pasting the changelog every time is tedious.
how_to_solve: Keep a live-updated context in NeverExplain called /recall-api and trigger it whenever you build new features.

keyword: avoid gpt-4o repetitive apologies and filler text
title: No More 'As an AI Language Model': Cutting the Fluff with Prompt Hooks
problem_description: Users hate the repetitive 'I apologize', 'Sure, I can help with that' filler text that consumes output tokens and time.
how_to_solve: A /recall-no-fluff hook adds instructions to 'be concise, skip the intro, and get straight to the code' automatically.

keyword: consistent seo rules for ai blog generation
title: Automating SEO Compliance: How to Feed Your Checklist to AI
problem_description: Content teams have to paste the same 15-point SEO checklist (slug rules, H1-H3 structure, keyword density) for every new article.
how_to_solve: Bind your SEO checklist to /recall-seo-rules to ensure every AI draft is pre-optimized.

keyword: explain complex codebase to new ai session
title: Contextual Deep-Dive: How to 'Onboard' AI to Your Legacy Codebase
problem_description: Starting a refactor on legacy code requires explaining the architecture, quirks, and technical debt every single time.
how_to_solve: Store a 'Technical Debt Map' in NeverExplain and use /recall-legacy to warn the AI about fragile parts of the code.

keyword: how to stop ai from using lucide-react in icons
title: Using FontAwesome or Custom Icons? Force AI to Stop Defaulting to Lucide
problem_description: AI models (especially v0 and ChatGPT) love Lucide-react, but many projects use FontAwesome or HeroIcons.
how_to_solve: Create a /recall-icons hook to specify exactly which icon library and import path to use.

title: Atomic Design with AI: Keeping Component Hierarchy Consistent
problem_description: AI often generates 'God components' instead of following Atomic Design principles (atoms, molecules, organisms).
how_to_solve: Use /recall-atomic to remind the AI to break down UI into your specific project structure.

keyword: sync prompts between web and mobile chatgpt
title: Cross-Device Prompt Sync: Access Your Project Context Everywhere
problem_description: Users find it hard to use complex project prompts when switching from Desktop to Mobile Chat.
how_to_solve: While the extension is desktop-first, NeverExplain's web dashboard allows quick copying of hooks for mobile use.

keyword: tell deepseek my code style for rust

problem_description: DeepSeek is powerful for coding but needs specific context for crate versions and safety preferences in Rust.
how_to_solve: Use /recall-rust-env to set up the perfect coding environment in DeepSeek instantly.

keyword: prevent ai from suggesting client-side logic in nextjs server components
title: Server Component Mastery: Stop AI from Putting 'use client' Everywhere
problem_description: AI struggles with the boundary between Server and Client components in Next.js, often adding 'use client' unnecessarily.
how_to_solve: A /recall-rsc hook provides the rules for when to use server vs client components in your specific architecture.

problem_description: Copywriters hate re-pasting 'Persona A: 30yo Tech Mom' and 'Persona B: 50yo CFO' descriptions for every ad variant.

keyword: automate jira ticket to pr description context

problem_description: The 'Notion -> Copy -> Tab Switch -> Paste' workflow is the #1 productivity killer for heavy AI users.
